Your Sales Flow — Do These In Order Every Day
1
→
Find Prospects
Open Prospect Finder → search niche + city → No Website filter → add Hot leads to pipeline. Goal: 20 before noon.
2
→
Audit Before You Call
Paste their website URL → get specific talking points → "I noticed your site isn't loading on mobile..." closes deals.
3
→
Generate Your Scripts
Import prospect from pipeline → generate personalized cold email, call script, and 5-touch follow-up in one click.

Send Proposal Same Day
Fill 6 fields → click Generate → send within 2 hours of every discovery call. Speed is your advantage. Most agencies take 5 days.
5
→
Onboard the Client

6
→
Send Monthly Reports

Google Voice — Call Center
Your Google Voice number is your business phone. Call prospects, receive inbound leads, record calls, and send texts — all from one free number. Scripts and tips below.
📱 Your Business Number
Save your Google Voice number here so it's always visible when you're calling prospects. Click "Open Google Voice" to launch your dialpad in a new tab.
Open Google Voice 📋 Call History 💬 Messages ⚙️ Settings
📋 Call Scripts — One Click to Copy
Use these word for word. The goal is NEVER to sell on the first call — it's to book a 5-minute Zoom or get them to look at the mockup.
Cold Call Opening (30 sec)
"Hey, is this [Name]? Hey — I'll be really quick. I was looking up [niche] in [city] and noticed you don't have a website — or it's not bringing in customers. I help [niche] get more [result] with a site built in 7 days. I even mocked something up for you. Takes 5 minutes to look at. Worth a quick Zoom this week?"
Voicemail (15 sec)
"Hey [Name], [Your Name] from Akira Digital. I found your business while searching [niche] in [city] — great reviews but no website showing up. I built a free mockup. Call me back at [your GV number]. Takes 5 minutes."
Follow-Up #1 (24 hrs after call)
"Hey [Name] — just circling back from our chat. I can hold that spot for 48 hours if you'd like to grab it. Let me know either way."
Follow-Up #2 (3 days later)
"Hey [Name] — quick check-in. Have you had a chance to look at the mockup? Happy to adjust anything at all — no pressure."
The Close Line (say on the call)
"If I can get this live in 7 days, would you want to move forward today? I can send you the payment link right now while we're on the call."
Text After No Answer
"Hey [Name], tried calling — Akira Digital re: your online presence. I built a free mockup of what your website could look like. Worth a quick look? [your GV number]"
⚡ Google Voice Pro Tips
Enable call recording: In Google Voice Settings → Calls → turn on "Incoming call options." Press 4 during any call to start recording. Review your closes and get better fast.
Get a local number: When setting up Google Voice, pick an area code that matches your city. Local numbers answer 35% more often than out-of-area calls.
Use the mobile app: Download Google Voice on your phone. When clients call your business number it rings your personal phone. You look like a real agency from day one.
Best calling hours: Tuesday–Thursday, 10AM–11:30AM and 2PM–4PM. These windows have the highest answer rates for small business owners. Avoid Monday mornings and Friday afternoons.
Set a voicemail greeting: "You've reached Akira Digital. We build high-converting websites for local businesses in 7 days. Leave your name and number and we'll call you right back." Sounds legitimate immediately.
Log every call in pipeline: After each call, immediately open Prospect Finder and log the touchpoint. Set the next follow-up date before you make the next call. Never trust your memory.
Limitation: Google Voice cannot auto-sync with your Akira Digital tools — but you can copy your GV call notes directly into the pipeline touchpoint log. Takes 30 seconds per call.
Client Flow — 11-Stage Pipeline
Every client moves through these exact stages. Set these up as your Notion Kanban board. Nothing falls through the cracks when everything has a stage and a time limit.
Rule: No payment = no build. No intake = no build. The build phase only starts after full payment AND completed intake form. Chasing content mid-build kills your schedule and margins.
Critical Time Rules
Proposal: Send within 2 hours of discovery call. Speed = credibility. Every hour you wait the client's excitement cools.
Welcome email: Send within 1 hour of payment clearing. Silence after payment kills trust immediately.
Build: Start within 24 hours of completed intake. Client sees progress in 48 hours — shows professionalism.
Care Plan pitch: Same day as launch, same conversation. Never wait to pitch it separately — momentum is everything.

Mercedes — AI Revenue Agent
AI Sales Agent · Outbound Prospecting + Follow-ups · Powered by Claude
Off Shift
Mercedes works your pipeline every weekday from 8am–5pm. For every lead she works, she researches the business, scores it as Hot / Warm / Cold, then generates: a personalized cold email + subject line, a 4-stage follow-up cadence (Day 3, 7, 14 & 30), a LinkedIn DM, an SMS script, a 30-second call opener, a proposal hook, and objection rebuttals — all tailored to that lead's industry, location, rating, and website situation. Hot and Day 7 warm leads also receive an automatic SMS via Twilio.

What Akira Digital Sells
We build professional websites for local service businesses. Our edge: we deliver in 10 days when competitors take 6 weeks, and we start at $1,000 — less than one missed job.

Your commission: $60/mo forever
The Care Plan is the most important upsell. A client on a $300/mo Care Plan pays you $60 every single month — without you doing anything. Land 10 Care Plan clients = $600/month passive income. Always pitch it at launch day.
⚡ Our Edge Over Competitors
1
Speed: 10-day delivery vs. 6 weeks from a traditional agency. Speed is our #1 sales argument.
2
Price: $1,000 for a professional site. One extra booked job pays for it completely.
3
Afterpay: 4 payments of $250 every 2 weeks. Removes the "can't afford it" objection instantly.
4
Simplicity: Client touches nothing. We handle everything and hand them something that just works.
Best Niches to Target
Focus on businesses with high job values, no website, and active Google or social presence. These close fastest.
NicheWhy They're PerfectAvg Deal
Plumbers / HVAC / ElectriciansEmergency service = high urgency. No website = invisible for every Google search.$1K–$2.5K
Roofers / General ContractorsBig-ticket jobs — one new job pays for the site many times over.$2.5K+
Law Firms (Personal Injury)Highest revenue per client. Understand digital marketing. Fast decisions. Pay $5K–$15K.$5K–$7.5K
Wedding / Event VenuesCompletely visual. No website = losing every couple searching online.$2.5K
Wellness / Beauty StudiosImage-driven. Lash studios, massage therapists, yoga studios rarely have websites.$1K–$2.5K
Musicians / CreativesAlmost never have websites. Portfolio-driven. Rely entirely on Instagram — easy pitch.$1K
Dental / ChiropracticProfessional services embarrassed by poor web presence. Good budgets.$2.5K–$5K
✅ Lead Qualification Checklist
Before spending time on a prospect, confirm they hit at least 3 of these 5 signals:
✓
Has Google reviews — proves paying customers exist
✓
No website OR site looks pre-2018 / broken on mobile
✓
Local service with high job value (not a $20 product)
✓
Active Facebook, Instagram, or Google Business presence
✓
Has responded to a review or posted recently — still active
Call Scripts
Use these word for word. The goal of the cold call is NOT to sell — it's to book a 5-minute Zoom. That's it.
Cold Call Opening (30 seconds max)
"Hey, is this [Name]? Hey — I'll be really quick, I promise." "I found [Business Name] on Google while looking for [niche] in [city]," "and I noticed you didn't have a website listed." "I help local businesses like yours get set up online so customers can" "actually find and book you — I even went ahead and mocked something up." "Takes five minutes to walk through. Is that worth a quick look this week?"
📱 Voicemail Script
"Hey [Name], this is [Your Name] from Akira Digital — super quick." "I found your business while searching for [niche] in [city]" "and noticed you don't have a website yet." "I built a quick mockup of what yours could look like — free to check out." "Give me a call back at [your number]. Have a great day."
💬 Cold DM (Facebook / Instagram)
"Hey [Name] — I came across [Business Name] while looking for [niche] in [city]." "You've got great reviews. Quick question — are you currently taking on new clients?"
After they reply yes →
"That's great to hear. I noticed you don't have a website listed —" "I help local [niche] businesses get found online and get more bookings." "I actually mocked something up based on your page — want a quick look? Totally free."
The one rule: Never pitch on the first message. Your first DM is always a genuine question about their business. The pitch comes only after they reply. First message that pitches gets ignored instantly.
Objection Handling
Every objection has a real fear behind it. Address the fear, not the words. These responses are tested and work.
They SayReal FearYour Response
"How much does it cost?" Price anxiety "I haven't quoted you yet — let me show you the mockup first. If you don't like it, no hard feelings at all."
"I already have someone working on it" Stalling "No worries — when it's live, I can help drive traffic to it. Mind if I check back in a month?"
"I get business from referrals" Doesn't see ROI "That's awesome — and a website doubles what referrals can do. Someone hears about you, Googles you, sees nothing... that's a lost job."
"I can't afford it right now" Budget concern "Totally understand. We do Afterpay — 4 payments of $250 every two weeks. The site pays for itself with one new booking."
"I'll think about it" Needs more value "Totally fair. Can I send you the mockup so you have something concrete to think about? No pressure at all."
"My nephew is building it" Family loyalty "Love that! If it stalls out, we're here. Most people come back after 3 months of waiting on family."
"I'm not tech savvy" Fear of complexity "You won't touch a thing — we handle everything and hand you something that just works."
"Send me an email" Getting rid of you "Absolutely. What's the best email? I'll send you the mockup too — takes 5 seconds to look at."
Discovery Call Structure (20 minutes)
Use this exact order every time. Consistency builds a repeatable close rate. Let them talk — people buy from people who listen.
The 5-Step Call Flow
1
Open (2 min): "Tell me about your business — how long have you been running it?" Let them talk. People trust people who listen first.
2
Pain (5 min): "Where are most of your new customers coming from right now?" → "Have you ever lost a job because someone couldn't find you online?" → "What would one extra job per month be worth to you?"
3
Reveal (5 min): "So based on everything you told me, here's what I put together..." → screen share the mockup → walk through page by page → "Does this feel like you? What would you change?"
4
Offer (5 min): "Here's how this works: [package] is [price]. full payment is due upfront before we begin. We deliver in 10 days guaranteed." Always include: "Plus $300/month for our Care Plan so you never have to think about your website again."
5
Close (3 min): "Do you want to move forward? I can send you the payment link right now." If hesitant: "What's the one thing holding you back?" — address it directly. If still hesitant: "Can I send you the mockup to look at tonight?"
The close line that works: "Do you want to move forward? I can send you the Stripe payment link right now while we're still on the call." Sending it while they're on the call converts 3× better than sending after.
Follow-Up Sequence
80% of deals close on follow-up #3–5, not the first touch. Never stop after one attempt. Use your Outreach Scripts tool to generate all 5 touches automatically.
5-Touch Sequence
TouchTimingMessage
Touch 124 hrs after call"Hey [Name] — just circling back. I can hold that spot for 48 hours if you'd like to grab it."
Touch 23 days later"Quick check-in — have you had a chance to look at the mockup? Happy to adjust anything at all."
Touch 31 week later"Hey — I'm taking on 2 more clients this month. Wanted to give you first right of refusal."
Touch 42 weeks later"Last check-in — if timing isn't right, totally fine. I'll reach back out in 60 days."
Revival60 days later"Hey [Name] — checking back like I said. Still looking to get the site sorted?"
Use the Outreach Scripts tool to generate all 5 touches in one click. Import the prospect from your pipeline → click "Follow-Up Sequence" → copy and send. Takes 2 minutes.
Daily Routine
Follow this every working day. Consistency beats talent every time. 10 touches before noon is the one non-negotiable rule.
9:00 AM
Open Prospect Finder → search 2 niches + city → add Hot leads to pipeline
Target: 20 new prospects
9:30 AM
Run Audit Tool on top 5 prospects → note the #1 issue for your call opener
Target: 5 audits done
10:00 AM
Make calls using cold call script → goal is booking a 5-min Zoom, not selling on the call
Target: 10 outreach touches
12:00 PM
Send emails/DMs to anyone who didn't answer using Outreach Scripts tool
Target: 10 email/DM sends
2:00 PM
Discovery calls + demos → use Proposal Generator → send proposal same day
Target: Proposals sent same day
4:00 PM
Log all touchpoints in pipeline → set follow-up dates → check tomorrow's follow-ups
Target: Pipeline fully updated
The one rule: 10 outreach touches before noon every single day. Calls, emails, DMs — all count. This single habit, done daily for 60 days, will generate more revenue than any strategy or tool in this playbook.
Facebook Outreach
Facebook is where local service businesses live. They run their entire operation from a Business Page — often with zero website. It's a warm environment that converts far better than cold calls when worked correctly.
🎯 Facebook Prospect Signals — Prioritize These
SignalWhat It Means
No website in About sectionPrimary target — zero web presence
Website listed but URL brokenGreat prospect — they tried, failed, and are motivated to fix it
Cover photo is logo or low qualityVisual-minded business — easy upgrade pitch
Last post within 30 daysActive — will actually see your message
"Responds quickly" badgeHigh reply likelihood — prioritize these first
5–50 Google reviewsEnough proof of business without being too large to care
💬 Facebook Messenger — 4-Touch Sequence
1
First Touch — No Pitch
"Hey [Name] — I came across [Business Name] while looking for [niche] in [city]." "You've got great reviews. Quick question — are you currently taking on new clients?"
2
After They Reply Yes
"That's great to hear. I noticed you don't have a website listed —" "I help local [niche] businesses get set up online so customers can find and book you directly." "I actually mocked something up based on your page — would you want to take a quick look? Totally free."
3
No Reply After 3 Days
"Hey [Name] — just following up on my last message." "I put together a free mockup of what a website could look like for [Business Name]." "Even if the timing isn't right, I'm happy to share it — zero pressure."
4
The Soft Close
"Happy to jump on a 10-minute call this week to walk you through it." "I work with a few [niche] businesses in [city] and the sites have been bringing them consistent new leads." "Worth a quick look? Here's my Calendly: [link]"
The one rule for Facebook DMs: Never pitch on the first message. Message 1 is always a genuine question about their business. The pitch only comes after they reply. A first message that pitches gets ignored instantly — every time.
📋 Cold DM — Instagram & LinkedIn
Instagram — Under 80 words, conversational
"Hey [Name] — found [Business Name] while searching [niche] in [city]. Love the work you're sharing! Quick question: are you getting enough leads from your online presence right now?"
LinkedIn — Professional, under 100 words
"Hi [Name] — I came across [Business Name] while researching [niche] businesses in [city]. You have impressive reviews. I help local service businesses build websites that convert searchers into booked jobs. I put together a quick mockup for your business — would you be open to a 5-minute call to take a look? No strings attached."
Legal Compliance
📋 The 7 Essential Contract Clauses
Include every one of these in every client contract. These are the clauses that save you when something goes wrong.
1
Scope of Work + Explicit Exclusions
Protects against scope creep — clients can't add pages/features and claim it was "part of the deal."
"Akira Digital agrees to design and deliver a website as described in the attached Project Brief (Exhibit A). Any features, pages, or functionality not listed in Exhibit A are explicitly excluded and may be subject to additional fees at $75–100/hr."
2
Payment Terms + Kill Fee
Protects against clients firing you after 80% of work is done and owing you nothing.
"Full payment is due before any work begins. If Client terminates after work has commenced, Client pays for all work completed at $150/hr with a minimum cancellation fee of 50% of the total project price."
3
Revisions Policy
Stops unlimited revision requests. Define what a "revision round" is.
"A revision round is defined as one consolidated set of feedback submitted within 5 business days. Revisions beyond included rounds billed at $75–100/hr. Revisions do not include new features or additional pages."
4
Client Content Deadline
Stops clients going silent for weeks then demanding 3-day delivery.
"Client agrees to provide all required content within 5 business days of kickoff. If content is not provided within 14 days, Akira Digital may reschedule and a $200 rescheduling fee will apply."
5
IP Transfer on Full Payment
Stops clients refusing to pay final invoice while keeping the live website.
"IP transfer occurs only upon full payment. If the final invoice is unpaid, the client does not legally own the site. Akira Digital retains the right to display the project in its portfolio."
6
Care Plan Recurring Terms
Stripe requires merchants to have clear cancellation terms displayed before charging.
"The Care Plan is month-to-month, billed on the 1st of each month. Either party may cancel with 30 days written notice. No refunds for partial months. Services suspended if payment fails for 7+ days."
7
Limitation of Liability
Stops a client blaming you for a slow month and suing for lost revenue.
"Akira Digital's total liability shall not exceed the total amount paid by Client. We are not liable for lost revenue, lost data, or business interruption. We do not guarantee specific search rankings, traffic levels, or lead volume."
🌐 Legal Pages Required on Your Website
PageWhy RequiredWhere to Get It
Privacy PolicyRequired by law if your contact form collects name or email. Also required by Google Analytics.freeprivacypolicy.com — free, 5 min
Terms of ServiceRequired by Stripe to accept payments. Must state your refund policy.termsofservicegenerator.net — free, 10 min
🚫 Scope Exclusions — Always Excluded Unless Agreed
Always ExcludedAvailable as Add-On
Copywriting / content creationAdditional pages beyond package ($200/page)
Logo design or brand identityRush delivery under 7 days ($300 premium)
Custom photographyCopywriting ($75–100/hr)
E-commerce / payment processingGoogle Ads setup and management ($500/mo)
Third-party software feesMonthly SEO content ($300/mo)
Revisions beyond included roundsCare Plan ($300/mo recurring)
Commission Structure
You earn 20% on every deal you personally close. Commissions are paid within 3 business days of each payment clearing in Stripe.
Your Earnings Per Deal

📋 Commission Rules
✓
Deposit commissions paid when client's deposit clears in Stripe
✓
Balance commissions paid when final payment clears
✓
Care Plan commissions paid on the 5th of each month for prior month's active clients you brought in
✓
Payment via Venmo, Cash App, PayPal, or Zelle — your choice
✓
Commission statement sent by 7th of each month
✗
No commission on deals that are refunded or charged back
Focus on Care Plans. Close 10 clients on the $300/mo Care Plan and you earn $600/month recurring — every month, without making a single additional sale. That's the path to financial freedom. Always pitch Care Plan at every launch.

⚙ API SETTINGS
Anthropic API Key (AI Features)
Powers AI email writing, call scripts & proposals. Get free at console.anthropic.com
Google Places API Key (Prospect Finder)
Finds real businesses near you. Get free at console.cloud.google.com → Enable Places API → Create Credentials → API Key
Notion Token (Database Sync)
Syncs leads & clients to Notion. Get at notion.so/profile/integrations → click your integration → Regenerate token
Airtable Token (Save Leads to Cloud ☁️)
This saves your leads so you can see them on ANY device. Get free at airtable.com
Airtable Base ID (Which spreadsheet to save to)
Current Status
